articles.title: Brazil Meat Left Out of Europe Suppliers List in Risk to Trade
articles.summary: Brazil is absent from a list of countries authorized to supply animal products to the European Union, presenting a trade risk to the world’s largest beef exporter.

articles.title: AI Boom Shows Top US Grid ‘Too Big to Function,’ Regulator Says
articles.summary: The chairman of the top US energy regulator has put America’s biggest power grid on notice, warning that its operator may be too big to function adequately amid the AI data center boom.

articles.title: Copper Rallies Above $14,000 a Ton, Nearing Fresh All-Time High
articles.summary: Copper jumped above $14,000 a ton, closing in on a record high as a rebound in Chinese demand and mounting supply risks outweigh concerns about the Iran war’s impact on global growth.

articles.title: Chip Stocks Sink as Inflation Woes Boost US Yields: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: A selloff in high-flying chipmakers drove stocks lower, with the market also falling alongside bonds after inflation data showed the impacts of energy disruptions stemming from the war in Iran.

articles.title: Russia Sees 2026 Oil Output Flat as Kyiv Steps Up Drone Strikes
articles.summary: Russia expects its oil production to remain flat in 2026 and grow modestly in the coming two years as its energy infrastructure comes under intense drone attacks from Ukraine.

articles.title: Emerging-Market Currencies, Stocks Fall as Iran Truce Hopes Dim
articles.summary: Emerging-market currencies and stocks dropped Tuesday on concern the ceasefire between the US and Iran is on the brink of collapse, while a slump in South Korean shares also weighed on sentiment.
